name: Two Higgs doublet model (2HDM)
summary: Extends the SM scalar sector with a second SU(2)_L doublet, giving 5 physical Higgs bosons (h, H, A, H+, H-) and additional CP-violating phases. The minimal renormalisable extension that admits spontaneous CP violation. Realises naturally in supersymmetric models (MSSM Higgs sector) and underlies many BSM constructions of electroweak baryogenesis and flavour physics.

addresses_puzzles.note: Provides additional CP-violating phases needed for electroweak baryogenesis and a strongly first-order phase transition in parts of parameter space. Combined with Sakharov sphalerons, can produce BAU at the EW transition.

touches_benchmarks.note: Decoupling limit reproduces SM Higgs couplings at the few-percent level; LHC Run 3 measurements constrain m_H, tan(beta) parameter space.
CP-violating 2HDM phases generate two-loop EDMs (Barr-Zee); current nEDM and electron-EDM bounds constrain CP-violating couplings.
Two-loop Higgs/heavy-pseudoscalar contributions to (g-2)_mu can accommodate the FNAL+BNL anomaly for specific tan(beta) and Higgs masses.
Charged Higgs contributions to b -> s gamma are tightly constrained: 2HDM-II gives m_H+ > 580 GeV (BMix global fit).

new_scales: heavy Higgs mass scale m_A, m_H, m_H+ ~ 100 GeV - few TeV
introduces.note: Higgs potential parameters: two VEVs (tan beta), four physical mass eigenstates, mixing angle alpha, Z_2-breaking lambda_5 / m_12^2.

history: Lee (1973), 'A theory of spontaneous T violation', Phys. Rev. D 8, 1226. Embedded in SUSY (Fayet 1976; Inoue-Kakuto-Komatsu-Takeshita 1982). Comprehensive 2HDM review (Branco-Ferreira-Lavoura-Rebelo-Sher-Silva 2011). LHC searches and EDM bounds drive the parameter-space exclusion.

references.citation: Lee, 'A theory of spontaneous T violation', Phys. Rev. D 8, 1226 (1973)
Branco, Ferreira, Lavoura, Rebelo, Sher, Silva, 'Theory and phenomenology of two-Higgs-doublet models', Phys. Rep. 516, 1 (2012)
